problem: What membrane material filters all contaminants from water at low energy cost?
mechanism: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure)
hypothesis: What membrane material filters all contaminants from water at low energy cost? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Two-dimensional. Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure) directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Accelerated degradation study with 20 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way. — Marcus Aurelius
